element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns movement mechanics through side-scrolling terrain navigation before combat intensifies
Unlimited ammo ensures player can experiment without resource anxiety
Simple enemies drop hearts for health restoration, establishing risk-reward loop

element_introduction_order.element: Door-based chamber system (shops, treasuries, hot springs)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players discover optional rooms; heart currency system introduced via defeated enemies

element_introduction_order.element: Power-ups (Protective Crystal, Flaming Arrows, Sacred Bow)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Conditional on health threshold; reward system for performance in god's chamber and training chamber
Distinct from horizontal platforming; introduces spatial axis complexity
Optional map/torch/pencil items provided; gatekeeper boss introduces pattern recognition

element_introduction_order.element: Boss encounters and gatekeeper pattern attacks
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Centurion ally summoned via mallet/destroyed statue adds strategic layer
Three treasures required for access to final stage; narrative progression marker
Horizontal scrolling contrasts with vertical Underworld; difficulty escalates
Returns to vertical axis with heightened enemy density and complexity
Fundamental genre shift; abandons platforming for bullet-hell style gameplay; automatic screen scroll removes player movement control

element_introduction_order.element: Final boss (Medusa) with complex attack patterns
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Culmination of all learned mechanics; requires all three sacred treasures for accessibility

typical_relief_triggers: Entering safe chambers (hot springs restore health)
Accessing shops and black markets (resource acquisition)
Defeating stage gatekeeper and obtaining sacred treasure
Transition between stages provides brief narrative respite
typical_tension_triggers: Entering new platforming areas with escalating enemy density
Fortress labyrinth navigation without map (optional tool creates uncertainty)
Vertical climbing sections with falling hazards
Health depletion below power-up thresholds (loss of upgrades)
Sky Temple auto-scrolling shooter final sequence with no safe zones
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Three-stage escalation pattern: Underworld introduces mechanics via vertical climb with moderate challenge; Overworld presents horizontal platforming with increased difficulty; Skyworld combines both axes with peak complexity; final Sky Temple stage abandons platforming entirely for sustained high-tension shooter gameplay. Each stage concludes with fortress labyrinth (exploration tension) followed by boss encounter (combat tension) and relief through treasure acquisition. Within levels, tension cycles through enemy clusters alternating with navigable gaps and chamber access points.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Doors lead to optional chambers (shops, treasuries, training, god's chamber, hot springs). Player choice to enter or bypass creates pacing flexibility. Mallet-destructible stone statues revealing Centurion allies indicate hidden strategic depth.
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Destroyed statues release Centurion flying ally for boss support; treasure chambers and training chambers accessed via doors contain progression rewards; black market contrasts with standard shops (negotiation mechanic in FDS version)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Underworld Stage 1 serves as tutorial through enemy density and hazard introduction. Vertical climbing establishes core platforming without overwhelming complexity. Simple enemies provide heart drops for health learning. Fortress introduction with optional map/torch reduces labyrinth punishment.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Overworld Stage 2 escalates horizontal platforming speed requirements. Enemy patterns become more coordinated. Fortress challenges increase with more complex room layouts. Player must manage power-up health thresholds strategically. Boss gatekeepers require pattern recognition.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Skyworld Stage 3 combines vertical and horizontal complexity simultaneously. Enemy density peaks. Fortress labyrinths become intricate with poor map utility. Boss difficulty increases noticeably. Player must have optimized power-up management and resource conservation.
notable_spikes: First fortress gatekeeper boss (introduction to boss pattern learning)
Transition to Overworld horizontal scrolling with speed-based obstacles
Skyworld Stage 3 fortress with combined hazard types and enemy clusters
Sky Temple Stage 4 genre shift to auto-scrolling shooter (mechanical paradigm change creates temporary re-learning curve)
Final Medusa boss encounter (requires all three sacred treasures; pattern complexity peak)

notable_level_patterns.description: Player climbs upward through platforming challenges with enemies and hazards distributed vertically. Ceiling and floor boundaries define vertical movement space. Enemies positioned to block ascent path or force decision-making between combat and evasion.

Traditional left-to-right side-scrolling level with speed-based obstacles and enemy swarms. Platforms vary in height and spacing. Horizontal continuity broken by door access points offering chamber detours.

Discrete room-based navigation without auto-scroll. Rooms connect via doorways requiring player to solve spatial puzzles. Enemy placement in rooms creates mini-encounters. Gatekeeper boss occupies final room. Optional tools (map, torch, pencil) reduce navigation friction.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All three stage fortresses (Underworld, Overworld, Skyworld)

notable_level_patterns.description: Mid-level optional doors branch into temporary chambers: shops (heart exchange), treasuries (items), training (power-up test), god's chamber (stats upgrade), hot springs (heal). Player decides engagement cost-benefit. Non-linear progression through selective chamber access.

Screen automatically advances rightward without player control. Player dodges incoming projectile patterns while firing arrows. Enemies stationary or following predictable arcs. No platforming; pure avoidance and attack rhythm. Culminates in Medusa boss fight.
